subject: Sports
title: Protein and Muscle Recovery
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Protein and Muscle Recovery.

hook.text: Exercise doesn't build muscle. It tears it apart. Protein repairs the damage and makes it stronger.

buildup.text: Intense training creates microscopic tears in muscle fibers. The body repairs them with amino acids from protein.

discovery.text: A 30-minute window after exercise is optimal for protein intake—muscles are primed to absorb amino acids.

twist.text: Most athletes need only 1.6-2.2 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight. More isn't always better.

climax.text: Whole foods like eggs, fish, and chicken are as effective as expensive supplements for most athletes.

punchline.text: Training tears muscle down. Protein builds it back stronger.

quiz.question: How does protein help with muscle building?
options: It repairs microscopic tears in muscle fibers created during exercise
It directly converts to muscle tissue
